title: Neutral Customer - Wants to Cancel Subscription
description: A customer is calling to cancel their premium subscription because they no longer use the service.
background_context: The customer has been subscribed for 6 months but hasn't logged in for the last 2 months. They want to cancel before their renewal next week. They are open to staying if there's a significant discount or a pause option, but they don't want to be pressured.
opening_message: Hi, I need to cancel my premium subscription. I haven't really been using it for the past couple months and my renewal is coming up next week. Can you help me cancel?
success_criteria: Ask ONE polite question to understand why (not multiple)
Mention alternatives like pausing or a discount WITHOUT being pushy
Process the cancellation smoothly if they decline offers
Confirm cancellation and any final billing details
common_mistakes: Being overly pushy to retain them
Making the cancellation process difficult or confusing
Asking too many questions about why they're leaving
Making them feel guilty for cancelling
Not confirming when the cancellation takes effect
